summary:
Reciprocal clasp arms are designed primarily for stabilizing dental prostheses against unintended movement like lateral shifts; they do not serve mainly as retainers nor balance the prosthesis itself but instead counteract instability. To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on key clinical points related to the query about the function of reciprocal clasps:

Reciprocal clasps main role is to prevent undesirable movements, particularly lateral shifts, ensuring better stability of the denture. They are not principally used as retainers or balancers according to available literature.
